title: Section 9
content: (1) The maritime boundaries between India and any State whose coast is opposite or adjacent to that of India in regard to their respective territorial waters, contiguous zones, continental shelves, exclusive economic zones and other maritime zones shall be as determined by agreement (whether entered into before or after the commencement of this section) between India and such State and pending such agreement between India and any such State, and unless any other provisional arrangements are agreed to between them, the maritime boundaries between India and such State shall not extend beyond the line every point of which is equidistant from the nearest point from which the breadth of the territorial waters of India and of such State are measured. (2) Every agreement referred to in sub-section (1) shall, as soon as may be after it is entered into, be published in the Official Gazette. (3) The provisions of sub-section (1) shall have effect notwithstanding anything contained in any other provision of this Act.
